title: Pairing, System Reasoning, Debugging, and Project Defense · Opportunity Transfer Studio
summary: A different candidate, role family, location, access need, employment history, constraint, and hiring process requires an independent transfer of pairing, system reasoning, debugging, and project defense into a changed multi-format technical defense packet. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of multi-format technical defense packet with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: Establish roles, turn-taking, accessibility, tool use, communication, disagreement, and shared success for a collaborative exercise.
Reproduce the symptom, bound the last-known-good state, rank hypotheses, run discriminating checks, repair the cause, and retain regression evidence.
Elicit stakeholders, workload, quality scenarios, data, trust, failure, cost, operations, and evolution before proposing components.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Pairing Contract
steps.instruction:
lab case — A candidate dominates a pair task, guesses at a bug, draws fashionable architecture, and claims a team project without separating personal work. The lab variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Pairing Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for establish roles, turn-taking, accessibility, tool use, communication, disagreement, and shared success for a collaborative exercise. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Pairing Contract, keep Pairing Contract connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A candidate dominates a pair task, guesses at a bug, draws fashionable architecture, and claims a team project without separating personal work. steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab Project Defense
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Project Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A candidate dominates a pair task, guesses at a bug, draws fashionable architecture, and claims a team project without separating personal work. The lab variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Defend personal contribution, team decisions, failures, accessibility, security, testing, operations, outcomes, assistance, and non-claims with inspectable evidence.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Lab Project Defense.
steps.why: Retrieving Project Defense through the concrete multi-format technical defense packet case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Lab Project Defense, keep Project Defense connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend personal contribution, team decisions, failures, accessibility, security, testing, operations, outcomes, assistance, and non-claims with inspectable evidence.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Lab Project Defense, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Project Defense precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Lab Project Defense, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A candidate dominates a pair task, guesses at a bug, draws fashionable architecture, and claims a team project without separating personal work. The lab variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Defend personal contribution, team decisions, failures, accessibility, security, testing, operations, outcomes, assistance, and non-claims with inspectable evidence.

For Defend the transferred model · Lab Project Defense, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: Speaking in “we” avoids the need to identify personal contribution.
